summary: Iter72 verification of 3 new feature sets (HR Letters no-AI Paste & Design + card CRUD + editable details; Documents folders/preview/share-email). Backend: 10/10 pytest passed (test_iter72_new_features.py). Frontend: all 6 documented flows verified end-to-end. FLOW 1 CRUD: created TEST warning_letter → red top border + red design button; duplicate produced '(copy)' card; PDF download triggered (experience_letter-TEST_CRUD_Renamed.pdf ~KB PDF); delete removed both cards. FLOW 2 No-AI designer: pasted multi-paragraph text with Subject/heading/bullets → body_html got 'OFFICIAL WARNING' chip + subject + <li> bullets + #DC2626 accent; live preview rendered; save persisted. FLOW 3 Details edit: switched template warning→experience → design btn recoloured to rgb(5,150,105) and card border-top confirmed rgb(5,150,105) green; name renamed correctly. FLOW 4 Documents folders: uploaded 2 TEST files (one in 'TEST Iter72 Folder', one bare); folder pill appeared with count; alpha showed folder badge; filter pill limited grid to 1 card; bulk-select+move moved beta into the folder and badge appeared. FLOW 5 Preview: text file content 'Hello, this is Iter72 test file ALPHA…' rendered inline in doc-preview-modal; download button visible. FLOW 6 Share via Gmail: (a) selected 2 files → share modal listed BOTH exact filenames → send → backend 400 + toast 'Gmail connection expired — reconnect in Settings' (EXPECTED); (b) folder-as-ZIP → modal showed 'TEST Iter72 Folder.zip (2 files inside)' → send → same expected 400+toast. Full cleanup: deleted both TEST docs + TEST letter + duplicate. Zero unexpected console errors (only the two documented 400s on share-email).
critical_code_review_comments: HrLetters.jsx is now 668 lines — approaching the 700-line split threshold. Consider factoring LetterEditor, NewLetterModal, SigBlock, QrModal, LetterheadSettingsModal into their own files under /pages/portal/hr-letters/ to keep the composer readable as more design templates are added.

Documents.jsx (557 lines) mixes list + upload + preview + share + comments + move. MoveModal, ShareEmailModal, PreviewModal, UploadModal, CommentsDrawer are self-contained and could easily be split into per-file components — low-risk refactor for maintainability.

Documents.jsx PreviewModal uses atob(data.content_base64) synchronously to decode text — fine for the current 10 MB cap but would freeze the tab for a large text file. Consider TextDecoder on a Uint8Array for large text streams (minor perf polish).
Documents.jsx onDownload creates a data-URL <a> and clicks it — this works but for very large files a Blob URL + URL.revokeObjectURL is more memory-friendly. Not blocking.

hr_letters_module.py _design_letter_html should ideally HTML-escape untrusted pasted text before wrapping in styled tags. Currently pasted text is inserted raw — while the endpoint is admin-only (PRIV set), any HTML in the paste box would be preserved as-is (could be a feature — but if not intended, add a sanitizer). Non-blocking; admin-only surface.
Toast on share-email correctly surfaces the backend detail 'Gmail connection expired — reconnect in Settings' — verified through Sonner. GOOD.
Card top-border color style is inline (borderTop: 3px solid <accent>) which prevents Tailwind tree-shaking issues — the correct approach given dynamic template colors. Good pattern.
seed_data_creation: All created + cleaned. Created and deleted: (1) 'TEST CRUD' warning→experience letter via UI + its '(copy)' duplicate, (2) '/api/hr/letters' TEST Iter72 letter + duplicate via API, (3) 'TEST_iter72_alpha.txt' + 'TEST_iter72_beta.txt' in folder 'TEST Iter72 Folder' via UI, (4) same pair via API. Zero test data remains. The 3 real existing letters (Anita Sharma, 2× UITest Iter53) and the 10 existing documents (spec.txt × 9 + Performance_Certificate(5).pdf in 'Test' folder) were left completely untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_iter72_new_features.py (2 classes: TestHRLettersNewFeatures + TestDocumentsFolderShare). Frontend selectors verified working: HR Letters — hrl-card-{id}, hrl-edit-{id}, hrl-pdf-{id}, hrl-dup-{id}, hrl-del-{id}, hrl-new, hrl-new-template/name/pos/submit, hrl-paste-text, hrl-design-btn (colored per template accent), hrl-body, hrl-save, hrl-back, hrl-details-toggle → hrl-details-panel with hrl-edit-name/pos/template, hrl-download. Documents — upload-doc-btn → doc-upload-folder + doc-file-input (HIDDEN — use set_input_files not click; do NOT wait_for_selector visible) + doc-save-btn + doc-upload-close; doc-folder-tabs / doc-folder-all / doc-folder-{name}; doc-card-{id}, doc-select-{id}, doc-folder-badge-{id}, doc-preview-{id}, doc-download-{id}, doc-delete-{id}, doc-thumb-{id}, doc-name-{id}. doc-bulk-bar → doc-move-btn → doc-move-modal (doc-move-pick-{folder} pills + doc-move-folder-name input + doc-move-confirm + doc-move-unfile). doc-share-selected / doc-share-folder → doc-share-modal (doc-share-attachment-list + doc-share-to + doc-share-subject + doc-share-message + doc-share-send). doc-preview-modal → doc-preview-body + doc-preview-download. Sonner toast surfaces 'Gmail connection expired — reconnect in Settings' verifiable via [data-sonner-toast]. Both share-email 400s are EXPECTED — do NOT flag as bugs.
rca_of_the_issue: No issues found. All 3 iter72 feature groups ship correctly: (a) POST /api/hr/letters/{id}/format returns themed body_html with template-specific chip + accent color and persists to DB; (b) PATCH /api/hr/letters/{id} accepts employee_name/position/department/ctc/template — persistence verified via GET; (c) POST /api/hr/letters/{id}/duplicate clones with '(copy)' suffix; (d) GET /api/hr/letters/{id}/pdf returns valid PDF magic bytes; (e) POST /api/documents accepts folder field; (f) POST /api/documents/move bulk-moves; (g) POST /api/documents/share-email correctly builds attachments/zip and fails only at the documented Gmail OAuth stage with a user-friendly 400 message that the frontend toast surfaces. Frontend CRUD icons on every card work; template switch dynamically recolors card border, editor design box and preview. The 400 error on /api/documents/share-email is the documented expected behavior in this environment.
